preamble: The shift from [ʌ] to [au] represents a diphthongization process where a back/central nucleus develops a high back rounded off-glide.

examples.note: Diphthongization of central/back vowels is common in many regional and social varieties of English.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
